claws are main hand only, which makes them rather limited, while centi longsword can (and most likely) will go in off hand, in whixh ratio is more important then speed, and the stats on it does not hurt either. so ,i say you got screwed with your deal, but that's just my opninion.P

Forget about the minor stats that the lamentation has, 10/21 roxxors 9/19... I traded a pair of kylong greaves and a kerlorek'dar spine razor for one of these weapons on prexus..
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2002 at 5:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252415060
Scholar
37 posts
i wish people would stop posting these rubbish calculations and confusing the new players who come here for real information.

The formula's for damage calculation of weapons are well known now, and the following is the case;

Strength affects damage as a multiplier....it is not "added" and therefore benefits all weapons proportionally.

The level based damage bonus is added AFTER after all base damage/weapon skill/strength calculations have been done. It is NOT added to base damage.

It is always the same for 1 handed weapons no matter what they're damage or delay. The only benefit with a faster weapon is that this fixed bonus is being applied at the faster rate i.e. 1.9 seconds for a lammy, 2.1 for the claws.

The fact is these 2 weapons are so close in damage output to make it not an issue. The lammy has the extra stats which make it more beneficial overall, but every second warrior i see has a lammy, so using these will make you stand out a little more, with very little downside.
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2002 at 12:22 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767079068
Sage
266 posts
10/21 doesnt "roxxor" 9/19. Unless you are a low level newbie, its actually quite a bit worse. As tomstrife points out, you have to consider the level bonus as well. Additionally, you must look at the impact of high strength on the max damage of the weapon.

For every 20 strength you add, you get the equivalent of 1 weapon damage (2 pts actual damage) added to the weapon, beginning with a base of 75 str.

Looking at a lvl 50 warrior, with 255 str (not unlikely, buffed), you get a level based 8 damage bonus, and strength based damage increase equal to a 9 bonus.

(8+9+10)/21 = 27/21 = 1.285
(8+9+9)/19 = 26/19 = 1.368

The one damage becomes almost irrelevant at this level, while the 2 delay is huge.
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply

RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 1:06 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768313462
52 posts
Actually...
more damage doesn't always = more aggro
for a Rogue who is in a group. Yes Evade works, but not all the time. That's why there are high damage high delay weaps for Rogues. I can go a full fight without having to Evade because the tank is hitting faster than me but I am also hitting for more. if your Taunt is VERY good you prolly wont have to worry about this. If it isn't good, you want the faster weap for the aggro. Just my 2 cents.

QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2001 at 6:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966077734
10 posts
Your doing your math backwards, you divide the delay my the damage.. Giving this one a ratio of 2.1 and the lammy 2.11 and the lower the ratio the better the weapon... means this beats lammy only barley...

Example: Sword#1 10damage 20 delay yeilds a ratio of 2.0

Sword#2 is 20damage 10delay wich is a ratio of 0.5

which would you rather have?

Edited, Wed Dec 26 18:42:56 2001
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Feb 07 2003 at 8:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592342702400
295 posts
Yeah when I'm looking at my damage output and considering new weapons I factor in bonuses and then determine damage per delay. Really useful, especially when comparing 2 1handers to a 2hander. Calc it all up assuming a reasonable dual wield rate and you know the dmg output.

So when I look at my etched steel baton, 14/28, I think of that as 14 base damage. I calc the bonuses and additional damage and come up with a projected damage per delay, or the equivalent of one tenth of my DPS before procs and secondary attacks.
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2002 at 10:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763140376
182 posts
hmmm
sword #1= 10/20
sword #2= 10/15

#1= 0.5 dmg per delay
#2= 0.666... dmg per delay

0.666 > 0.5
dunno, it's just my way of comparing weaps.

# May 16 2004 at 5:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592257799818
25 posts
IF YOU USE THE SECOND SWOARD YOU GO TO HELL, 666 IS THE SIGN OF THE BESAT!!!1one1
▲ Page top
RE: this > lammy
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2002 at 5:55 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Some of you people really need to take some fundamental math courses.

Damage divided by Delay yields amount of damage per one delay point. The higher the result the better.

Going reverse, Delay divided by Damage yields length of time to do one damage point. The lower the number the better.

So ratio-wise, Spined Dragon beats Lamentation.

Edited, Wed Feb 20 17:58:20 2002
